span.p: Edge-case inset — authorship is content-level, not container-level

span.p:
embedded AI-generated span
carries its OWN disclosure block
at the embed point:
mode_1_edge_case_flag = true

span.p:
Exhaustive enumeration —
exactly three values.
No fourth mode.

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
mode-1
Mode 1 — Human-Led, AI-Enforced
(human author of record)

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
mode-2
Mode 2 — AI-Led, Human-Reviewed
(AI author + named human reviewer)

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
mode-1-with-embedded-mode-2-summary
Mode 1 with the embedded-Mode-2-summary edge case
(human-authored container,
AI-authored embedded sub-outputs)

div.style: color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(26, 26, 26) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
A fourth mode routes through the
section 4.10 amendment process,
NEVER a runtime workaround
